display.details: Marjorie Evelyn Clemans (b. ~1912) married Homer Mundell in 1933; her own parents are not established, and four same-name candidates have been excluded (see {{link:#case.07|case.07}}). The one traceable Clemans family in her marriage district — Robert M. Clemans (b. ~1869), a widower boarding at Flagler in 1920 (the town where Evelyn was born), whose sons Robert Earl and Jasper William farmed at Merino, Logan County — was reconstructed across the 1900–1940 censuses and does not contain her: Robert M. was widowed with his children already raised by their grandparents by 1900, Earl was a childless newlywed in 1920, and Jasper's recorded children are only a daughter by his second wife. Only Jasper's undocumented first marriage (about 1909) remains an unresolved gap. With no Marjorie Clemans in any Colorado or Kansas census from 1920 to 1940 and no grave, death, or remarriage record located, her origin is unrecovered from the online record; the remaining paths are offline Colorado vital records or her own later-life documents.

Not yet proven. Cecilia and Leonard's memorials name Catherine as Cecilia's mother but do not extend her parentage.{{cite:src.cecilia-zodrow-zimmerman-find-a-grave.5586b816}}{{cite:src.leonard-ferdinand-zodrow-find-a-grave.7870a1b4}} {{case:case.23}} carries Catherine's parent search without reopening the completed Cecilia correction; note that the Fleckenstein family already touches this tree through Martha Zodrow's marriage to a Fleckenstein.
display.identity: Doyle's open Fleckenstein Gen 4 slot through Catherine.

label: Competing parentage of John Laborn Ford

display.details: Still open. Catherina's maiden name is now consistently appearing as Koeberger/Koburger, but I do not yet have a county, parish, or European locality for her parents.

label: Earlier Long, Walker, McClelland, Love, Hoge, and James ancestors
Earlier Mundell, Cantwell, Rupert, and Rust ancestors

display.details: Open pending original county records, cemetery files, probate, and church/civil records. Mary Frances's parents are now established as John F. Rust and Ethel B. Rupert; the remaining Rust gap is the proposed step from F. John to John Aissen Rust and Frauke Folkerts Everwyn.
display.title_override: Earlier Brown, Elmore, Cantwell, Rupert, and Rust ancestors

display.details: Likely born roughly 1800-1820. The Archion Band 25 family register and Band 15 marriage book should be the next records.
display.title_override: Unknown German ancestors above John Paul and Catherine

display.details: Not yet proven. Best target: Archion Mainhardt Taufregister 1835-1843 Band 7, if John was baptized in that parish.
Not yet proven. Best target: the 1864-ish marriage entry and Mainhardt family register.

label: Earlier Zodrow, Grams, and Fleckenstein ancestors

display.details: No direct public proof located yet. Search should focus on {{case:case.23}} for Catherine Fleckenstein's parents; Sheridan County, Kansas; Marquette and Shawano County, Wisconsin Zodrow records; Idaho death certificates for Zodrow siblings; Lebehnke Catholic records and Polish archive land files for Neu Lebehnke in Kreis Deutsch Krone; and Polish/German Grams parish variants.

Open but useful. Lebehnke banns name Albert Zodrow of Alt Lebehnke as son of Christoph Zodrow and Rosalia Zaske of Neu Lebehnke; another entry names August Zodrow as son of the deceased Christoph Zodrow and Rosalia Zaske; other entries point to Andreas Zodrow with Justina Reinke and to additional Zodrow daughters. These are not John's parents yet, but they are the right neighborhood and record set: Neu Lebehnke / Nowa Lubianka for the village, Lebehnke / Stara Lubianka for the parish and civil registry, and Kreis Deutsch Krone for county-level records.
display.identity: Doyle's Zodrow collateral cluster; not yet an ancestor row.
